name: Maxine Sullivan
abstract: Maxine Sullivan (May 13, 1911 – April 7, 1987), born Marietta Williams in Homestead, Pennsylvania, United States, was an American jazz vocalist and performer. As a vocalist, Sullivan was active for half a century, from the mid-1930s to just before her death in 1987.

quotes.text: of 'Loch Lomond.' So Jimmy Mundy took the arrangement off the record. And when they came to New York and did the concert at Carnegie Hall, and Martha Tilton sang it, you know, they thought they were introducing somebody, and I was singing it on second speed. So that got to be a big joke too, you know.
quotes.caption: Recalling Martha Tilton singing 'Loch Lomond' at Carnegie Hall

quotes.text: when Benny did this first swing concert at Carnegie Hall, he thought he was introducing Loch Lo-mond." And when Martha Tilton began singing, a lot of the fans in the audience knew that I had been doing it on 52nd Street for -- well, I guess for about six or seven months by that time.
quotes.caption: Recalling Benny Goodman's 1938 Swing Concert

quotes.text: Al Cobb is an arranger, and at one time, he was -- In fact, I did an appearance with him at Carnegie Hall. And he had transcribed the arrangements of 'If I Had A Ribbon Bow' and 'Molly Malone' and a couple of the Kirby things off the
quotes.caption: Maxine Sullivan recalls performing with arranger Al Cobb

quotes.text: This was a concert that we did at Carnegie Hall for the New York City of Visiting Nurses.
quotes.caption: Maxine Sullivan recalls a benefit concert for Visiting Nurses

quotes.text: Yes. He has his own club, and I think he does a little promoting. I think he bring a lot of American artists over there. He mentioned the fact that he had caught the Carnegie Hall concert. And he liked 'I've Got The World On A String.'
quotes.caption: Speaker recalls someone else attending a concert

about_quotes.speaker: Glenn Zottola
about_quotes.evidence: Multi-instrumentalist Glenn Zottola has enjoyed a diverse career in music that includes collaborations with Bob Wilber, Benny Goodman, and Maxine Sullivan.
about_quotes.doc_title: Glenn Zottola interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 7, 2020

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie Williams
about_quotes.evidence: I had the great pleasure of working with one of the great ladies actually, Miss Maxine Sullivan, and in fact I did a few recordings with Maxine.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie Williams interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, October 18, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Cliff Leeman
about_quotes.evidence: No, he had been divorced. He had been married two or three times by then.

about_quotes.speaker: Russell Procope
about_quotes.evidence: Kirby was looking for someone to play Maxine's music... I played the set with Maxine and it went over pretty big, you know. So Kirby, he hired me on the spot right then.

about_quotes.speaker: Keith Ingham
about_quotes.evidence: I worked a lot with a wonderful jazz singer, Maxine Sullivan. And I did three albums with Maxine
about_quotes.doc_title: Keith Ingham interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Ralph Sutton
about_quotes.evidence: I remember working at the Village Vanguard behind Maxine Sullivan, who I adored.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ralph Sutton interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, September 22, 1995
